name: pravo-logika
description: Formal logic of legal reasoning on Russian law: legal norm structure, subsumption (applying a norm to facts as a syllogism), interpretation methods, the law/fact distinction, and logical unambiguity of contract wording. Modes: legal review (pravo-logika:review), argument fixing, clause-check, comparison of two opposing positions (pravo-logika:compare), and norm analysis (pravo-logika:norm). Not a source of law — statutes and case law are flagged for human verification, never asserted from memory. Builds on the classical logic engine distilled from G. Chelpanov's textbook.
